The standard rewards guessing from familiar words.
using a rule from a different unit
source credibility is useful when students verify sources.
questions.explanation: This answer connects a concept to a skill, which is the central move in standards-aligned review.

questions.courseLabel: High School Computer Science and Digital Fluency
standardRefs: NYS Digital Fluency
New York State P-12 Learning Standards
questions.prompt: For Media Literacy, which response sounds most like a high-quality constructed response?
questions.correctText: It names source credibility, cites evidence, and explains the connection.
choices: making a claim without supporting evidence
It names source credibility, cites evidence, and explains the connection.
choosing an answer because it uses familiar words
It lists three vocabulary words without a claim.
questions.explanation: Constructed responses need a claim, evidence, and explanation, whether the item is NYS, AP, or standards-aligned classroom practice.

questions.prompt: Which review target belongs most directly in Media Literacy?
questions.correctText: misinformation
choices: constraint
credential
copyright
misinformation
questions.explanation: misinformation is part of the concept cluster for Media Literacy, so it belongs in this unit rather than in an unrelated review bucket.

questions.courseLabel: High School Computer Science and Digital Fluency
standardRefs: NYS Digital Fluency
New York State P-12 Learning Standards
questions.prompt: A student wants to transfer Media Literacy knowledge to a new question. What is the best strategy?
questions.correctText: Match the new prompt to source credibility, then justify the answer with evidence.
choices: ignoring the source or data in the prompt
Ignore unfamiliar data or wording.
describing a topic without explaining why it matters
Match the new prompt to source credibility, then justify the answer with evidence.
questions.explanation: Transfer requires recognizing the underlying concept and then adapting it to the new prompt.

questions.courseLabel: High School Computer Science and Digital Fluency
standardRefs: NYS Digital Fluency
New York State P-12 Learning Standards
questions.prompt: High School Computer Science and Digital Fluency - Media Literacy: which pair best shows how two ideas in the unit connect?
questions.correctText: source credibility connects to algorithmic feed.
choices: making a claim without supporting evidence
choosing an answer because it uses familiar words
source credibility connects to algorithmic feed.
copyright replaces all evidence.
questions.explanation: The pair source credibility and algorithmic feed belongs together in Media Literacy; students should explain the relationship rather than memorize each term alone.

questions.courseLabel: High School Computer Science and Digital Fluency
standardRefs: NYS Digital Fluency
New York State P-12 Learning Standards
questions.prompt: Which answer best describes the role of data table, diagram, or model in a High School Computer Science and Digital Fluency question on Media Literacy?
questions.correctText: It provides evidence students must use before deciding.
choices: It only matters if it repeats the vocabulary word.
It provides evidence students must use before deciding.
using a rule from a different unit
memorizing an isolated fact without evidence
questions.explanation: The data table, diagram, or model is part of the task. Students should read it for evidence, patterns, constraints, or context before selecting an answer.

questions.courseLabel: High School Computer Science and Digital Fluency
standardRefs: NYS Digital Fluency
New York State P-12 Learning Standards
questions.prompt: Which statement would be easiest to defend in Media Literacy?
questions.correctText: source credibility can be explained with evidence from data table, diagram, or model.
choices: source credibility can be explained with evidence from data table, diagram, or model.
ignoring the source or data in the prompt
algorithmic feed does not need evidence.
describing a topic without explaining why it matters
questions.explanation: Defensible answers connect a course concept to evidence from the task. That is the common pattern behind NYS, AP, and classroom review questions.

questions.courseLabel: High School Computer Science and Digital Fluency
standardRefs: NYS Digital Fluency
New York State P-12 Learning Standards
questions.prompt: A student misses a NYS standards-aligned classroom task on Media Literacy. Which feedback would help most?
questions.correctText: Reread the task, identify source credibility, and explain the evidence step by step.
choices: making a claim without supporting evidence
choosing an answer because it uses familiar words
Memorize more unrelated vocabulary before trying again.
Reread the task, identify source credibility, and explain the evidence step by step.
questions.explanation: Useful feedback turns the missed item into a process: read the task, identify the concept, and explain evidence.
